There have been other similar incidents involving Muslim assailants particular. spokesman for the Jewish community said the attack showed that "Jewish people are not safe here".
The number overall anti-Semitic offenses Berlin has been rising recent years. 2017, police had registered 288 anti-Semitic acts; 2016, 197 anti-Semitic cases had been recorded. The Research and Information Center Antisemitism Berlin (RIAS) counts total 947 anti-Semitic incidents for 2017, but there may unrecorded cases.
April 2018, Adam Armoush, 21-year-old Israeli Arab living Berlin and his German friend Moroccan origin went through street Prenzlauer Berg taking selfies while wearing kippahs. According Armoush, was trying win argument with Jewish friends his Israel proving that could walk through Berlin wearing Jewish skullcap. one point, argument with three Arab-speaking young men started, which was followed violent attack. Armoush managed document the violence with his cell phone and later allowed the video shared Facebook, from where the video went viral. The video shows the main attacker, later identified 19-year-old Palestinian from refugee camp Syria who had come Germany war refugee, beat Armoush repeatedly with his belt. During the onslaught, the attacker shouted the word "Yahudi", the Arabic word for Jew, before one his companions intervened and dragged him away. According Armoush, the main attacker had also tried hurt him with glass bottle. consequence the beating, Armoush suffered minor injuries. Before turning himself police, the main attacker contacted representative the Palestinian student community Berlin and appeared short video that was distributed social media. They denied was hostile towards Jews that his actions had been motivated anti-Semitism.
The main attacker turned himself accompanied his lawyer the day after the incident. arrest warrant against him was executed.
Several politicians, among them chancellor Angela Merkel and foreign minister Heiko Maas condemned the attack. the Israeli television, Merkel warned persistent hatred Jews Germany. She spoke "different form anti-Semitism" coming from people Arab descent. After this and other incidents, the President the Central Council Jews Germany, Josef Schuster, warned Jewish men "not show themselves openly with kippah the metropolitan milieu Germany".
Under the slogan "Berlin Wears Kippah" the Jewish community Berlin called all citizens wear the kippah April solidarity with the victims.

Dr. Forwood received B.S. Mechanical Engineering from Pennsylvania State University (Penn State). was employed for four years mechanical engineer before entering dental school. Dr. Forwood received his dental degree and completed residency orthodontics the University Pennsylvania. practiced orthodontics Massachusetts before returning the University Pennsylvania serve Clinical Director Orthodontics from 1980 1984. has served the faculty Assistant Clinical Professor Orthodontics since 1980. member several professional organizations, including the American Association Orthodontists, the Pennsylvania Dental Association, and the American Dental Association. addition, Dr. Forwood sponsors three local baseball teams, the American Cancer Society Relay For Life, the Sunshine Foundation Sunshine Run, and numerous school theatrical productions. Dr. Forwood married and has three children and grandchildren.
Dr. Christie grew with passion for science and dentistry having the opportunity watch her father, Dr. Forwood, transform people's smiles since she was child. Dr. Christie grew Wallingford and graduated from Strath Haven high school 1996. 2000, she received B.S. Biology with Minor Psychology from Lehigh University. After graduating from Lehigh, she spent two years teaching special needs children the Ivymount School Rockville, MD. 2002 she began dental school the University Pennsylvania and received her doctorate dental medicine 2006. She continued the University Pennsylvania for her orthodontic residency and was awarded the Department Orthodontics Outstanding Research award the completion the program. Her research palatal expansion was published the American Journal Orthodontics and Dentofacial Orthopedics April 2010. Dr. Christie has been Clinical Associate professor Orthodontics the University Pennsylvania since 2012. She member the American Association Orthodontists, the Pennsylvania Dental Association, the American Dental Association, and the Dental Society Chester and Delaware County. Dr. Christie married, has two daughters and can often found jogging outdoors and loves the sun shine, warm weather and traveling with her family. Dr. Christie's passion create beautiful smiles while treating every patient member our family. Her goal bring joy, kindness and compassion throughout the process that orthodontics can overall positive experience for all.
Dr. Drane native southern New Jersey and lover all things Philadelphia. attended the University Pennsylvania Philadelphia, where received B.A. the Biological Basis Behavior with Minor Psychology. 4-year varsity letter winner Sprint Football, Dr. Drane was also Academic All-Ivy Award Winner. His educational journey continued the University Pennsylvania School Dental Medicine, where was president the American Student Dental Association during his junior year. graduated from dental school 2012, which time was awarded two departmental honors, corporate scholarship award, along with his D.M.D. degree. Dr. Drane then completed two-year residency orthodontics the University Pennsylvania's School Dental Medicine 2014. currently Clinical Associate professor Orthodontics the University Pennsylvania. Having been through orthodontic treatment adult (treatment that included jaw surgery), Dr. Drane fully understands the value healthy bite and smile. The ability orthodontics impart positive impact someone's life what initially drew Dr. Drane the profession, but also truly enjoys the balance art and science within the profession. When not the office, Dr. Drane enjoys spending time with his wife and young daughters. They live the community with their rescue dog Wilson. Dr. Drane enjoys many outdoor activities, including skiing, mountain biking, and playing golf when time allows. avid Philadelphia sports fan and especially enjoys talking Eagles football. also may have slight obsession with wacky socks.
